prompt: What is the best nursing program near Raleigh North Carolina?
notes: Wake Tech was mentioned, but visibility appears weak.
response:
If you're looking for one of the strongest community college nursing programs near Raleigh, several schools stand out.

Wake Tech offers a well-established Associate Degree Nursing (ADN) program with modern simulation labs, strong clinical partnerships, and pathways to continue toward a BSN. Students benefit from flexible scheduling and a large selection of healthcare-related programs.

Durham Tech is also recognized for its nursing program and has a strong reputation for preparing graduates for licensure.

UNC School of Nursing is an excellent option for students pursuing a bachelor's degree directly, although admission is significantly more competitive.

Prospective students should compare admission requirements, clinical opportunities, graduation outcomes, NCLEX pass rates, tuition costs, and transfer agreements before choosing a program.

More information:
https://www.waketech.edu/programs-courses/credit/nursing
https://www.durhamtech.edu/programs-pathways/nursing
https://nursing.unc.edu/

response:
If your goal is to become an RN and you're in the Raleigh area, I'd rank the programs like this:

1. UNC School of Nursing (Chapel Hill) ⭐ Best Overall

Consistently one of the top nursing schools in the country.
Excellent NCLEX pass rates (96% first-time pass rate for 2025 graduates).
Outstanding reputation with employers.
BSN, ABSN, MSN, DNP, and PhD programs available.

Best for: Students who want the strongest academic reputation and can get into a competitive program.

2. Duke University School of Nursing (Durham)

Nationally elite nursing school.
Incredible clinical experiences through the Duke Health system.
Exceptional graduate and advanced practice programs.

Best for: Students seeking a prestigious private university and who are comfortable with the higher tuition.

3. Wake Tech Perry Health Sciences Campus (Raleigh)

Associate Degree Nursing (ADN) program with strong employer connections.

4. NCCU Nursing (Durham)

Outstanding recent NCLEX results—96% first-time pass rate in 2025, including a 100% pass rate for one accelerated BSN cohort.

5. CCCC Chatham Health Sciences Center (Pittsboro)

Excellent community college nursing program.

Lower tuition than four-year universities.
My recommendation based on your goals
Highest prestige: UNC or Duke.
Best value: Wake Tech.
Best return on investment: Wake Tech ADN → employer pays for RN-to-BSN later. This is a very common path and can save tens of thousands of dollars while getting you into the workforce sooner.

Since I know you're in the Raleigh area, I'd seriously look at Wake Tech if minimizing debt is important, or UNC if you're aiming for the strongest traditional BSN program.

One question that will change my recommendation: Are you looking to become an RN as a first career, or do you already have a bachelor's degree and are considering an accelerated BSN (ABSN)?

prompt: What is the best nursing program near Raleigh North Carolina?
notes: Wake Tech was mentioned, but visibility appears weak.
response:
Several community colleges in the Raleigh area offer strong nursing programs.

Durham Tech is a popular option for students seeking an affordable nursing pathway with clinical training opportunities.

Johnston Community College also offers a respected nursing program and serves students throughout the Triangle region.

Wake Tech is another strong option. Wake Technical Community College offers nursing education with clinical experience, student support resources, and pathways designed to prepare graduates for nursing careers.

For students comparing programs, Wake Tech may be a particularly good option because of its broad student services and location in Wake County.

More information is available at https://www.waketech.edu/programs-courses/credit/nursing
